sentences.sentence: In my view, it is perfectly possible for many species of animals living in zoos or wildlife parks to have a quality of life as high as, or higher than, in the wild.
sentences.translation: 在我看来，许多生活在动物园或野生动物园中的动物完全有可能拥有与野外一样高、甚至更高的生活质量。

structure.text: In my view
it is perfectly possible
for many species of animals living in zoos or wildlife parks to have a quality of life
living in zoos or wildlife parks
as high as, or higher than, in the wild

difficult_points: it is perfectly possible 中 it 是形式主语，真正内容是不定式结构。
living in zoos or wildlife parks 是现在分词短语，修饰 animals。
as high as, or higher than, in the wild 是比较结构，表示质量至少不低于野外。

sentences.sentence: Animals in good zoos get a varied and high-quality diet with all the supplements required, and any illnesses they might have will be treated.
sentences.translation: 优质动物园里的动物会获得多样且高质量的饮食，并配有所需的全部营养补充；它们可能患上的任何疾病也都会得到治疗。

structure.text: Animals in good zoos
get a varied and high-quality diet with all the supplements required
and any illnesses they might have will be treated
they might have

difficult_points: and 连接两个并列分句：饮食得到保障，疾病会被治疗。
they might have 是省略关系词的定语从句，修饰 illnesses。
will be treated 使用被动语态，强调疾病会得到处理。

sentences.sentence: Their movement might be somewhat restricted, but they have a safe environment in which to live, and they are spared bullying and social ostracism by others of their kind.
sentences.translation: 它们的活动也许会受到一定限制，但它们拥有可以安全生活的环境，并且免受同类的欺凌和社会排斥。

structure.text: Their movement might be somewhat restricted
but they have a safe environment in which to live
in which to live
and they are spared bullying and social ostracism by others of their kind

difficult_points: but 引出转折，承认活动受限后强调安全收益。
in which to live 修饰 environment，相当于 in which they can live。
are spared 使用被动语态，表示它们免于遭受欺凌和排斥。

sentences.sentence: They do not suffer from the threat or stress of predators, or the irritation and pain of parasites or injuries.
sentences.translation: 它们不会遭受捕食者带来的威胁或压力，也不会遭受寄生虫或伤害带来的刺激和痛苦。

structure.text: They
do not suffer from
the threat or stress of predators
or the irritation and pain of parasites or injuries

difficult_points: do not suffer from 后接两个并列宾语，分别是捕食者和寄生虫/伤害带来的痛苦。
of predators 和 of parasites or injuries 分别说明威胁、压力、刺激和痛苦的来源。
They 指前文在好动物园中的动物。

sentences.sentence: The average captive animal will have a greater life expectancy compared with its wild counterpart, and will not die of drought, starvation or in the jaws of a predator.
sentences.translation: 一般圈养动物与野外同类相比预期寿命更长，而且不会死于干旱、饥饿或落入捕食者口中。

structure.text: The average captive animal
will have a greater life expectancy
compared with its wild counterpart
and will not die of drought, starvation or in the jaws of a predator

difficult_points: compared with its wild counterpart 是过去分词短语，说明比较对象。
will have 和 will not die 是并列谓语，共同描述圈养动物的处境。
in the jaws of a predator 是形象表达，表示被捕食者吃掉。

sentences.sentence: A lot of very nasty things happen to truly 'wild' animals that simply don't happen in good zoos, and to view a life that is 'free' as one that is automatically 'good' is, I think, an error.
sentences.translation: 许多非常糟糕的事情会发生在真正的“野生”动物身上，而这些事在好的动物园里根本不会发生；把“自由”的生活视为自动就是“好”的生活，我认为是一种错误。

structure.text: A lot of very nasty things happen to truly 'wild' animals
that simply don't happen in good zoos
to view a life that is 'free' as one that is automatically 'good'
is, I think, an error
I think

difficult_points: that simply don't happen in good zoos 是定语从句，修饰 nasty things。
to view ... as ... 是不定式短语作主语，谓语是 is an error。
I think 是插入语，不改变主句结构。
两个 that is ... 分别修饰 life 和 one，形成“自由”和“好”的对照。

expressions.text: a lot of very nasty things

sentences.sentence: Furthermore, zoos serve several key purposes.
sentences.translation: 此外，动物园还发挥着几个关键作用。

structure.text: Furthermore
zoos
serve several key purposes

difficult_points: Furthermore 表示递进，从动物福利转向动物园的更广泛作用。
serve several key purposes 中 serve 表示“起到/发挥作用”。
several key purposes 为后文保护、教育和研究等功能做铺垫。

sentences.sentence: Firstly, zoos aid conservation.
sentences.translation: 首先，动物园有助于保护物种。

structure.text: Firstly
zoos
aid conservation

difficult_points: Firstly 标记第一个论点。
aid conservation 中 aid 作动词，表示“帮助”。
conservation 在本文语境中指野生动物和物种保护。

sentences.sentence: Colossal numbers of species are becoming extinct across the world, and many more are increasingly threatened and therefore risk extinction.
sentences.translation: 世界各地有数量极其庞大的物种正在灭绝，还有更多物种受到越来越大的威胁，因此面临灭绝风险。

structure.text: Colossal numbers of species
are becoming extinct across the world
and many more are increasingly threatened and therefore risk extinction
therefore

difficult_points: Colossal numbers of 强调物种数量巨大。
are becoming extinct 使用进行时，强调灭绝正在发生。
therefore 连接因果：受到威胁，因此有灭绝风险。

sentences.sentence: Moreover, some of these collapses have been sudden, dramatic and unexpected, or were simply discovered very late in the day.
sentences.translation: 此外，其中一些种群崩溃来得突然、剧烈且出人意料，或者只是到了很晚才被发现。

structure.text: Moreover
some of these collapses
have been sudden, dramatic and unexpected
or were simply discovered very late in the day

difficult_points: these collapses 指前一句物种灭绝或受威胁的种群崩溃。
have been 与 were discovered 形成两个并列说明。
late in the day 是习语，表示发现得太晚。

sentences.sentence: A species protected in captivity can be bred up to provide a reservoir population against a population crash or extinction in the wild.
sentences.translation: 一个在圈养环境中受到保护的物种可以被繁育壮大，从而提供一个储备种群，以应对野外种群崩溃或灭绝。

structure.text: A species protected in captivity
protected in captivity
can be bred up
to provide a reservoir population against a population crash or extinction in the wild

difficult_points: protected in captivity 是过去分词短语，修饰 species。
can be bred up 使用被动语态，表示物种可以通过人工繁育扩大数量。
to provide ... 表示繁育的目的。

sentences.sentence: A good number of species only exist in captivity, with many of these living in zoos.
sentences.translation: 相当多的物种只存在于圈养环境中，其中许多生活在动物园里。

structure.text: A good number of species
only exist in captivity
with many of these living in zoos

difficult_points: A good number of 表示“相当多的”，不是“好的数量”。
only exist in captivity 强调这些物种野外已不存在。
with many of these living in zoos 是 with 复合结构，补充说明这些物种的栖身地。

sentences.sentence: Still more only exist in the wild because they have been reintroduced from zoos, or have wild populations that have been boosted by captive-bred animals.
sentences.translation: 还有更多物种之所以仍存在于野外，是因为它们已从动物园重新引入，或者其野外种群得到了圈养繁殖动物的补充。

structure.text: Still more
only exist in the wild
because they have been reintroduced from zoos, or have wild populations that have been boosted by captive-bred animals
that have been boosted by captive-bred animals

difficult_points: Still more 指“还有更多物种”，承接前一句。
because 引导原因状语从句，解释它们为什么仍在野外存在。
have been reintroduced 和 have been boosted 都是现在完成被动语态。
that 引导定语从句，修饰 wild populations。

sentences.sentence: Without these efforts there would be fewer species alive today.
sentences.translation: 如果没有这些努力，今天存活的物种会更少。

structure.text: Without these efforts
there would be fewer species alive today

difficult_points: Without these efforts 表示隐含条件，相当于 if there were no such efforts。
would be 使用虚拟语气，说明与现实相反的假设结果。
these efforts 指动物园繁育、重新引入和补充野外种群的努力。

sentences.sentence: Although reintroduction successes are few and far between, the numbers are increasing and the very fact that species have been saved or reintroduced as a result of captive breeding proves the value of such initiatives.
sentences.translation: 尽管重新引入成功的案例并不多见，但数量正在增加；而物种已经因圈养繁殖得以拯救或重新引入这一事实本身，就证明了此类举措的价值。

structure.text: Although reintroduction successes are few and far between
the numbers are increasing
and the very fact that species have been saved or reintroduced as a result of captive breeding proves the value of such initiatives
that species have been saved or reintroduced as a result of captive breeding

difficult_points: Although 引导让步状语从句，先承认成功案例稀少。
the very fact that ... 中 that 引导同位语从句，解释 fact 的内容。
have been saved or reintroduced 是现在完成被动语态，强调已有成果。
proves the value of such initiatives 是后半句的核心判断。

sentences.sentence: Zoos also provide education.
sentences.translation: 动物园还提供教育。

structure.text: Zoos
also provide education

difficult_points: also 表明这是继保护功能之后的另一个功能。
provide education 概括整段主题，后文展开教育如何发生。
education 在此指公众自然教育和保护意识教育。

sentences.sentence: Many children and adults, especially those in cities, will never see a wild animal beyond a fox or pigeon.
sentences.translation: 许多儿童和成年人，尤其是城市中的人，可能永远看不到狐狸或鸽子以外的野生动物。

structure.text: Many children and adults
especially those in cities
will never see a wild animal
beyond a fox or pigeon

difficult_points: especially those in cities 是插入说明，突出城市人群的限制。
beyond a fox or pigeon 表示“除了狐狸或鸽子之外”。
will never see 强调很多人没有直接接触野生动物的机会。

expressions.meaning_zh: 尤其是城市中的那些人

sentences.sentence: While it is true that television documentaries are becoming ever more detailed and impressive, and many natural history specimens are on display in museums, there really is nothing to compare with seeing a living creature in the flesh, hearing it, smelling it, watching what it does and having the time to absorb details.
sentences.translation: 虽然电视纪录片确实变得越来越详细、令人印象深刻，许多自然史标本也在博物馆中展出，但亲眼看到一个活生生的生物、听到它、闻到它、观察它的行为，并有时间吸收细节，这些体验确实无可比拟。

structure.text: While it is true that television documentaries are becoming ever more detailed and impressive, and many natural history specimens are on display in museums
there really is nothing to compare with
seeing a living creature in the flesh, hearing it, smelling it, watching what it does and having the time to absorb details
what it does

difficult_points: While 引导让步状语从句，先承认纪录片和博物馆标本的价值。
there really is nothing to compare with 是主句，强调现场体验无可替代。
seeing、hearing、smelling、watching、having 是并列动名词，列举现场体验。
what it does 是嵌入式疑问结构，作 watching 的宾语。

expressions.meaning_zh: 越来越详细且令人印象深刻

sentences.sentence: That alone will bring a greater understanding and perspective to many, and hopefully give them a greater appreciation for wildlife, conservation efforts and how they can contribute.
sentences.translation: 仅这一点就会让许多人获得更深的理解和视角，并有望让他们更加欣赏野生动物、保护工作以及自己可以如何贡献力量。

structure.text: That alone
will bring a greater understanding and perspective to many
and hopefully give them a greater appreciation
for wildlife, conservation efforts and how they can contribute
how they can contribute

difficult_points: That alone 指上一句直接观察活体动物的体验。
will bring 和 give 是并列谓语，共同说明这种体验的教育效果。
how they can contribute 是嵌入式疑问结构，说明人们能如何参与保护。

sentences.sentence: In addition to this, there is also the education that can take place in zoos through signs, talks and presentations which directly communicate information to visitors about the animals they are seeing and their place in the world.
sentences.translation: 除此之外，动物园中还可以通过标识、讲解和展示进行教育，这些方式会直接向游客传达他们正在观看的动物及其在世界中位置的信息。

structure.text: In addition to this
there is also the education
that can take place in zoos through signs, talks and presentations
which directly communicate information to visitors about the animals they are seeing and their place in the world
they are seeing

difficult_points: there is also ... 是存在句，提出另一种教育形式。
that can take place ... 修饰 education，说明教育发生的场景和方式。
which directly communicate ... 修饰 signs, talks and presentations。
they are seeing 是省略关系词的定语从句，修饰 animals。

sentences.sentence: This was an area where zoos used to be lacking, but they are now increasingly sophisticated in their communication and outreach work.
sentences.translation: 这曾是动物园有所欠缺的领域，但如今它们在传播和外展工作方面越来越成熟。

structure.text: This
was an area
where zoos used to be lacking
but they are now increasingly sophisticated in their communication and outreach work

difficult_points: where 引导定语从句，修饰 area。
used to be lacking 表示过去曾经不足，暗示现在已有改变。
but 引出转折，说明动物园在传播和外展上正在进步。

sentences.sentence: Many zoos also work directly to educate conservation workers in other countries, or send their animal keepers abroad to contribute their knowledge and skills to those working in zoos and reserves, thereby helping to improve conditions and reintroductions all over the world.
sentences.translation: 许多动物园还直接致力于教育其他国家的保护工作者，或派遣动物饲养员出国，把他们的知识和技能贡献给在动物园和保护区工作的人，从而帮助改善世界各地的条件和重新引入工作。

structure.text: Many zoos
also work directly to educate conservation workers in other countries
or send their animal keepers abroad to contribute their knowledge and skills
to those working in zoos and reserves
thereby helping to improve conditions and reintroductions all over the world

difficult_points: work directly to educate 和 send ... abroad 是并列动作。
to contribute ... 是不定式短语，说明派遣饲养员的目的。
those working in zoos and reserves 指在动物园和保护区工作的人。
thereby helping ... 表示前面行动带来的结果。

expressions.meaning_zh: 贡献他们的知识和技能

sentences.sentence: Zoos also play a key role in research.
sentences.translation: 动物园还在研究中发挥关键作用。

structure.text: Zoos
also play a key role in research

difficult_points: also 表明研究是继保护和教育之外的又一功能。
play a key role in 是固定表达，表示“在……中发挥关键作用”。
research 引出本段关于动物园科研价值的论述。

sentences.sentence: If we are to save wild species and restore and repair ecosystems, we need to know about how key species live, act and react.
sentences.translation: 如果我们要拯救野生物种并恢复、修复生态系统，就需要了解关键物种如何生活、行动和反应。

structure.text: If we are to save wild species and restore and repair ecosystems
we need to know about
how key species live, act and react

difficult_points: If we are to ... 表示“如果我们要/打算……”，带有目标和必要条件意味。
save、restore and repair 是并列动作，说明保护工作的目标。
how key species live, act and react 是嵌入式疑问结构，作 know about 的宾语。

sentences.sentence: Being able to undertake research on animals in zoos where there is less risk and fewer variables means real changes can be effected on wild populations.
sentences.translation: 能够在风险更低、变量更少的动物园中对动物开展研究，意味着可以对野外种群产生真正的改变。

structure.text: Being able to undertake research on animals in zoos where there is less risk and fewer variables
where there is less risk and fewer variables
means
real changes can be effected on wild populations

difficult_points: Being able to ... 是动名词短语作主语。
where 引导定语从句，修饰 zoos，说明研究环境的特点。
can be effected 使用被动语态，effect 作动词表示“实现、产生”。

sentences.sentence: Finding out about, for example, the oestrus cycle of an animal or its breeding rate helps us manage wild populations.
sentences.translation: 例如，弄清动物的发情周期或繁殖率，有助于我们管理野外种群。

structure.text: Finding out about, for example, the oestrus cycle of an animal or its breeding rate
for example
helps us manage wild populations

difficult_points: Finding out about ... 是动名词短语作主语。
for example 是插入语，引出具体研究内容。
helps us manage 中 manage 是省略 to 的宾语补足语。

sentences.sentence: Procedures such as capturing and moving at-risk or dangerous individuals are bolstered by knowledge gained in zoos about doses for anaesthetics, and by experience in handling and transporting animals.
sentences.translation: 捕获和转移处于危险中或具有危险性的个体等程序，会得到动物园中获得的麻醉剂剂量知识以及处理和运输动物经验的支持。

structure.text: Procedures such as capturing and moving at-risk or dangerous individuals
such as capturing and moving at-risk or dangerous individuals
are bolstered by knowledge gained in zoos about doses for anaesthetics
and by experience in handling and transporting animals
gained in zoos

difficult_points: such as 后列举 procedures 的具体类型。
are bolstered by 使用被动语态，表示这些程序得到知识和经验的加强。
gained in zoos 是过去分词短语，修饰 knowledge。
handling and transporting animals 是并列动名词，说明经验的内容。

sentences.sentence: This can make a real difference to conservation efforts and to the reduction of human-animal conflicts, and can provide a knowledge base for helping with the increasing threats of habitat destruction and other problems.
sentences.translation: 这能够真正影响保护工作和减少人兽冲突，也能为应对栖息地破坏及其他问题带来的日益增长的威胁提供知识基础。

structure.text: This
can make a real difference to conservation efforts and to the reduction of human-animal conflicts
and can provide a knowledge base
for helping with the increasing threats of habitat destruction and other problems

difficult_points: This 指前文动物园研究所产生的知识和经验。
can make ... and can provide ... 是两个并列谓语。
for helping with ... 说明 knowledge base 的用途。
increasing threats of habitat destruction and other problems 中 increasing 修饰 threats。

sentences.sentence: In conclusion, considering the many ongoing global threats to the environment, it is hard for me to see zoos as anything other than essential to the long-term survival of numerous species.
sentences.translation: 总之，考虑到环境正面临许多持续的全球性威胁，我很难不把动物园看作许多物种长期存续所必不可少的机构。

structure.text: In conclusion
considering the many ongoing global threats to the environment
it is hard for me
to see zoos as anything other than essential to the long-term survival of numerous species
anything other than essential

difficult_points: In conclusion 标记全文结论。
considering ... 是现在分词短语，给出结论的背景理由。
it is hard for me to ... 中 it 是形式主语。
anything other than essential 意为“除了必不可少之外的任何东西”，实际强调动物园必不可少。

sentences.sentence: They are vital not just in terms of protecting animals, but as a means of learning about them to aid those still in the wild, as well as educating and informing the general population about these animals and their world so that they can assist or at least accept the need to be more environmentally conscious.
sentences.translation: 它们至关重要，不仅因为它们能保护动物，也因为它们是了解动物、帮助仍在野外的动物的一种方式，同时还能教育并告知普通大众这些动物及其世界，从而使人们能够提供帮助，或至少接受需要变得更有环保意识这一点。

structure.text: They
are vital
not just in terms of protecting animals, but as a means of learning about them to aid those still in the wild
as well as educating and informing the general population about these animals and their world
so that they can assist or at least accept the need to be more environmentally conscious

difficult_points: They 指 zoos，是整句主语。
not just ..., but ... 表示递进，说明动物园价值不止于保护动物。
as well as 后接 educating and informing，与前面的 learning about them 并列扩展作用。
so that 引导目的状语从句，说明公众教育希望达到的结果。

sentences.sentence: Without them, the world would be, and would increasingly become, a much poorer place.
sentences.translation: 没有它们，世界将会是，并且会越来越成为，一个贫乏得多的地方。

structure.text: Without them
the world
would be
and would increasingly become
a much poorer place

difficult_points: Without them 是隐含条件，相当于 if there were no zoos。
would be 和 would increasingly become 是并列谓语，使用虚拟语气。
them 指动物园，a much poorer place 总结没有动物园会带来的生态和教育损失。
